brand.subtitle: Load, edit and generate Authelia configuration files
switch.advancedTitle: Show non-critical fields and the raw YAML editor
lang.label: Language
ui.theme.label: Appearance
rules.title: Rules
rules.subtitle: (evaluated in order, first match wins)
rules.hint: Basic editing recreates the rule list from the fields below. For advanced rule keys (methods, query, etc.) use Advanced configuration.
oidc.hint: OpenID Connect provider. The signing key (jwks), named authorization_policies, claims_policies, custom scopes, cors and custom lifespans are preserved but only editable via Advanced configuration (raw YAML).
oidc.clientsHint: Registered OIDC clients. Unmanaged per-client keys (per-client jwks, *_encrypted_response_alg/enc, *_signed_response_key_id, endpoint auth signing algs, etc.) are preserved when editing — use Advanced YAML to set those.
client.label: Client #{n}
client.name: Client name
client.hashConfirm: The value already looks like a hash. Hash it again?
help.theme: Web UI theme of the Authelia portal (light, dark, grey or auto).
help.logLevel: Verbosity of Authelia logs. Use debug/trace only for troubleshooting.
help.serverAddress: Address and port Authelia listens on, e.g. tcp://:9091.
help.sessionName: Name of the session cookie. Default: authelia_session.
help.domain: Root domain the session cookie is valid for (e.g. example.com).
help.autheliaUrl: Public URL of the Authelia portal for this domain.
help.expiration: How long a session stays valid (e.g. 1h).
help.inactivity: Idle time before the session expires (e.g. 5m).
help.rememberMe: Duration of the 'remember me' option; 0 to disable it (e.g. 1M).
help.defaultPolicy: Policy applied when no rule matches: deny, bypass, one_factor or two_factor.
help.oidc.hmacSecret: Secret used to sign OIDC tokens. Use a long random string.
help.oidc.enforcePkce: Whether PKCE is required: never, public_clients_only or always.
help.oidc.debug: Include detailed OIDC errors in client responses (debugging only).
help.oidc.lifespanDevice: Lifetime of device codes for the device authorization grant (e.g. 10m).
help.oidc.discoveryAlg: Signing algorithm advertised for OpenID Connect discovery responses.
help.oidc.discoveryKeyId: Key ID (from jwks) used to sign discovery responses.
help.oidc.minParamEntropy: Minimum entropy required for the state/nonce parameters; -1 disables the check.
help.oidc.pkcePlain: Allow the insecure 'plain' PKCE challenge method (not recommended; prefer S256).
help.oidc.statelessIntrospection: Use stateless JWT access tokens for introspection instead of the database.
help.oidc.requirePar: Require all clients to use Pushed Authorization Requests (PAR).
help.rule.domains: Domain(s) this rule applies to; comma-separated for multiple.
help.rule.policy: Access level for matches: deny, bypass, one_factor or two_factor.
help.rule.subject: Restrict to users/groups, e.g. group:admins or user:john.
help.rule.resources: Regex on the path to restrict the rule, e.g. ^/api/.*$.
help.rule.networks: Restrict the rule to source networks/IPs (CIDR).
help.client.id: Unique identifier of the OIDC client (client_id).
help.client.name: Friendly name shown on the consent screen.
help.client.secret: Shared secret (hashed). Use the Hash button for a plaintext value.
help.client.authPolicy: Authentication required for this client: one_factor or two_factor.
help.client.redirectUris: Allowed callback URLs after login; comma-separated.
help.client.scopes: Scopes the client can request (openid, profile, email, groups).
help.client.public: Enable for public clients (SPA/CLI) without a secret.
help.client.grantTypes: OAuth2 grant types, e.g. authorization_code, refresh_token.
help.client.tokenAuth: Client authentication method at the token endpoint.
help.client.requirePkce: Force PKCE for this client (recommended for public clients; some apps like Proxmox require it).
help.client.pkceMethod: PKCE challenge method; S256 is the secure choice, plain is discouraged.
help.client.accessTokenAlg: Signing algorithm for access tokens; 'none' means opaque tokens (the usual choice).
help.client.userinfoAlg: Signing algorithm for userinfo responses; usually 'none'.
help.client.consentMode: How consent is asked: auto, explicit, implicit, or pre-configured (remembers the decision).
help.client.consentDuration: How long a pre-configured consent stays valid (e.g. 1y, 1w). Only used with consent mode 'pre-configured'.
help.client.responseModes: How the authorization response is delivered: query, fragment or form_post.
help.client.idTokenAlg: Signing algorithm for the ID token; must be a real signature (no 'none').
help.client.authzAlg: Signing algorithm for JARM authorization responses; 'none' disables JARM.
help.client.audienceMode: How the requested audience is matched: explicit or implicit.
help.client.audience: Additional audiences (aud claim) this client can request tokens for.
help.client.lifespan: Name of a custom lifespan profile defined under identity_providers.oidc.lifespans.custom.
help.client.claimsPolicy: Name of a custom claims policy defined under identity_providers.oidc.claims_policies.
help.client.sectorId: URL hosting a list of redirect URIs, used to compute pairwise subject identifiers.
help.client.requestUris: Allow-listed URIs the client can reference in a 'request_uri' parameter.
help.client.jwksUri: URL where Authelia fetches this client's public keys (mutually exclusive with a client-specific jwks list).
help.client.revocationAuth: Client authentication method at the token revocation endpoint.
help.client.introspectionAuth: Client authentication method at the token introspection endpoint.
help.client.multiAuth: Allow this client to authenticate with more than one method at once.
help.client.requirePar: Require this specific client to use Pushed Authorization Requests (PAR).
help.user.username: Login username (the map key in users_database.yml).
help.user.displayname: Full name shown in the UI.
help.user.email: User email, used for notifications and password reset.
help.user.groups: Groups the user belongs to; comma-separated.
help.user.newPassword: Type a new password to (re)set it; hashed with argon2id. Leave empty to keep the current one.
help.user.disabled: If checked, the account cannot log in.
users.hint: To set/change a password, type it in the New password field: it will be stored as an argon2id hash. If left empty, the existing hash is kept.
advanced.title: Raw YAML
advanced.hint: Edit any detail here. Press Apply to forms to re-read the basic values from this YAML.
advanced.placeholder: Load a file or paste the YAML here...
output.title: Generated file
rule.label: Rule #{n}
rule.subject: Subject (e.g. group:admins)
user.label: User #{n}
